description: Dreamy, lo-fi indie pop with warm textures, mellow vibes, and introspective lyrics
suno_tags: indie pop, dreamy lo-fi, warm synths, mellow vocals, reverb guitar, chill, nostalgic, 95 BPM
vocal_style: Soft, slightly breathy, and effortlessly cool. Not trying too hard — like singing to yourself on a late-night walk. Understated delivery with occasional moments of gentle intensity. Gender-neutral tone works best.

lyrics_guidelines.vocabulary_level: observational, poetic but accessible, nostalgic, gentle
tips: Write like a diary entry set to music — personal but not confessional
Use quiet, everyday imagery: streetlights, rain on windows, 2 AM walks
Chorus can be softer than verse — indie doesn't need a big pop hook
Leave space for the instruments — not every beat needs a word
Nostalgia is your best friend — 'remember when' energy

example_lyrics:
[Intro]
[Effect: Lo-fi]
[Instrumental]

[Verse 1]
Sidewalks wet from afternoon rain
Your voice still lives inside my phone
I scroll past your name sometimes
Wondering if you do the same

I wear your city like a bruise
Beautiful but tender still

[Chorus]
We were golden for a summer
Figured out the world at three AM
Now I'm just a little older
Still not sure where that went

[Verse 2]
The coffee shop replaced its chairs
The barista doesn't know my name
I guess that happens when you leave
The world just fills the space

I found your bookmark in my shelf
Page forty-two, you never finished

[Chorus]
We were golden for a summer
Figured out the world at three AM
Now I'm just a little older
Still not sure where that went

[Bridge]
[Whispered]
I'm not sad, I'm just remembering
How everything can change
From permanent to passing
From a name to just a page

[Chorus]
We were golden for a summer
Figured out the world at three AM
Now I'm just a little older
Still not sure where that went

[Outro]
[Effect: Lo-fi]
[Instrumental]
[Effect: Fade Out]
[End]
do: Embrace imperfection — slightly raw production is the aesthetic
Use reverb and lo-fi effects to create dreamy atmosphere
Let the lyrics breathe — not every line needs to connect directly
Mix conversational lines with poetic ones
End with an extended instrumental outro that fades naturally
dont: Don't write a big pop chorus — indie choruses can be understated
Don't over-polish — the charm is in the rough edges
Don't use club or party language — this is introspective territory
Don't force rhymes — natural speech patterns over forced structure
Don't add too many instruments — less is more in indie pop
